entities.name: OSTROM'S SOCIO-ECOLOGICAL SYSTEMS FRAMEWORK
entities.description: A framework used as a structured diagnostic to define systemic problems and avoid linear thinking.

entities.name: MEADOWS'S LEVERAGE POINT HIERARCHY
entities.description: A hierarchy used as a structured diagnostic to define systemic problems and avoid linear thinking.

entities.name: LINEAR THINKING
entities.description: A simplistic approach to problem solving that the frameworks aim to avoid.

entities.name: IRRIGATION SCHEME
entities.description: A water management system analyzed for systemic dysfunction and transition.

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The country where the analyzed irrigation scheme is located.

entities.name: BASELINE CONDITION
entities.description: The initial state of the irrigation scheme used for ex post analysis.

entities.name: INTERVENTION OUTCOMES
entities.description: The results of interventions applied to the irrigation scheme.

entities.name: LEVERAGE POINTS
entities.description: Strategic points in a system where interventions can initiate systemic change.

entities.name: FEEDBACK MECHANISMS
entities.description: Processes that drive leverage points and contribute to systemic change.

entities.name: INSTITUTIONAL DESIGN
entities.description: The structural arrangement of institutions that drives leverage points.

entities.name: SYSTEMIC CHANGE
entities.description: The process of shifting a system from a dysfunctional state to a functional one.

entities.name: COMPLEX ADAPTIVE SYSTEMS
entities.description: The desired state of transitioned dysfunctional irrigation schemes.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL INNOVATION PLATFORMS
entities.description: Tools used to identify systemic challenges and intervention points for transition.

entities.name: SYSTEMIC CHALLENGES
entities.description: Problems within the irrigation scheme identified through innovation platforms.
